page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon core running at 2.8 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average single-core performance but struggles under multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU test and single-threaded gzip compression both score in the average middle 50% tier. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 10% category. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read and write speeds for large blocks registering in the bottom 10%.

Due to its low memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, the instance presents a specific resource density tradeoff. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU cycles but have minimal memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and simple background processing, whereas memory-heavy databases, large-scale multi-threaded compilation, and LLM inference are constrained by the hardware profile.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor and 2.0 GB of system memory. It contains 1 physical core with 2 threads and does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression performance, but multi-core operations and memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 10% tier. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks with low memory requirements, such as lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and simple utility scripting, while rendering it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. It offers average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-2 features 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM, optimized for low-memory, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance with minimal memory footprint, designed for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic utility processing.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
2.0 GB of system memory with a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
